channel.title: Frontion Tier 1 Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Trump announces three-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire with prisoner swap
item.description: Donald Trump announced a three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, including a 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner swap, after both sides accused each other of violating separate ceasefires around Victory Day celebrations.

item.title: 🇬🇷 Greece investigates mystery naval drone found in Ionian Sea
item.description: Greek military is examining an unmanned naval drone with explosives discovered by fishermen near Lefkada. Reports suggest it may be Ukrainian-made and linked to attacks on Russia's "shadow fleet" bypassing Western sanctions.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iran accuses US of reckless military adventure as Hormuz clashes continue
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accused the US of opting for military action whenever diplomacy is on the table, as both sides exchanged attacks in the Strait of Hormuz and the US enforced a naval blockade of Iranian ports.

item.title: 💻 Major cyber attack disrupts thousands of universities and schools worldwide
item.description: Hacking group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for an attack on Canvas academic software, affecting approximately 9,000 institutions globally. Students faced exam cancellations and coursework disruptions across US, Canada, Australia and Europe.

item.title: 🇿🇦 South Africa Constitutional Court orders Ramaphosa impeachment review
item.description: South Africa's Constitutional Court ruled that parliament violated the constitution by blocking impeachment proceedings against President Cyril Ramaphosa over the Phala Phala farm scandal. Opposition leader Julius Malema called for his resignation.

item.title: 🇿🇦 South African Opposition Demands Ramaphosa Resign After Court Ruling
item.description: Julius Malema's Economic Freedom Fighters party challenged parliament's 2022 decision to block impeachment proceedings against President Cyril Ramaphosa over the Phala Phala farm scandal, where $580,000 in cash was stolen from his private game farm.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel-Lebanon talks to continue as US presses Hezbollah disarmament
item.description: US mediators are pushing for Hezbollah disarmament as part of ongoing Israel-Lebanon negotiations, with the Trump team seeking to reduce tensions along the border.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran's ambassador to China says US cannot turn Beijing against Tehran
item.description: Iran's ambassador to China expressed confidence that Washington cannot drive a wedge between Tehran and Beijing, as the two countries maintain strong economic and diplomatic ties despite US pressure.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US-Iran War: Hormuz blockade and naval operations continue
item.description: Channel News Asia reports on the ongoing US naval blockade of Iranian ports and military operations in the Strait of Hormuz, where 20% of world oil transits. US proposals remain under negotiation as Iran considers its response.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇮🇹 Meloni meets Rubio as Iran war strains Italy-US ties
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni met with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio as tensions over the Iran conflict create friction between Italy and its NATO ally over trade and energy security concerns.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan parliament approves extra defense spending below government request
item.description: Taiwan's legislature passed additional defense spending to strengthen military capabilities against Chinese pressure, though less than the government had requested. The move comes amid heightened tensions across the Taiwan Strait.

item.title: 🇺🇳 US revises UN resolution on Iran; China, Russia expected to veto
item.description: The United States has revised its proposed UN Security Council resolution on Iran, but China and Russia are still expected to veto the measure, continuing the diplomatic standoff over Iran policy.

item.title: 🇬🇧 British MPs visit China for first time in seven years
item.description: A delegation of British Members of Parliament visited China for the first time in seven years, marking a potential thaw in diplomatic relations as both countries seek to maintain dialogue amid global uncertainties.

item.title: 📈 European shares retreat as Middle East tensions flare
item.description: European stock markets declined as tensions in the Middle East escalated, with investors concerned about the impact of the Iran conflict on global energy supplies and economic stability.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of violating ceasefire
item.description: Both Russia and Ukraine accused each other of ceasefire violations around Victory Day celebrations, with Moscow reporting drone attacks and Kyiv reporting continued shelling despite declared truces.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine says it is running short on air defence missiles
item.description: Ukrainian officials warned that air defence missile stocks are running dangerously low, as Russia continues its aerial bombardment campaign and Victory Day approaches.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Divided Kingdom: Pro-independence parties surge across Britain
item.description: Support for Scottish independence and other pro-independence movements has surged across Britain, according to new polling, as political divisions deepen ahead of potential elections.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Labour suffers defeat in UK local elections as Reform gains ground
item.description: The Labour Party faced significant losses in UK local elections, with Nigel Farage's Reform Party making gains as voters expressed frustration with the government's economic and immigration policies.

item.title: ⛪ Vatican marks one year of Pope Leo
item.description: The Vatican commemorated one year since Pope Leo was elected to lead the Catholic Church, the first American pontiff in history.

item.title: 🇭🇰 Two men convicted in Hong Kong trade office spying case
item.description: A UK court convicted two men linked to the Hong Kong trade office on espionage charges, marking another case in the ongoing tensions between the UK and China over national security.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iraq's Sadr says exclude armed groups from next government as pressure builds
item.description: Influential Iraqi cleric Muqtada al-Sadr called for armed groups to be excluded from Iraq's next government, as political pressure builds over the formation of a new administration.

item.title: 🇳🇴 Norway police hold Chinese woman over suspected espionage
item.description: Norwegian police arrested a Chinese woman on suspicion of espionage, continuing a pattern of Nordic countries cracking down on suspected intelligence activities.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US-Iran conflict: Major developments on May 8, 2026
item.description: Key developments in the ongoing US-Iran conflict, including military operations, diplomatic negotiations, and regional impacts.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China signals Taiwan priority ahead of Trump-Xi meeting
item.description: China reaffirmed that Taiwan remains at the core of its interests ahead of the Trump-Xi summit scheduled for May 14-15, with Foreign Ministry stating US adherence to the One China principle is essential for stable relations.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine launches major drone attack on Russia ahead of Victory Day
item.description: Russia's Defense Ministry said air defenses shot down 347 Ukrainian drones overnight in one of Ukraine's largest aerial attacks, targeting Moscow ahead of Victory Day celebrations. The attack disrupted flights and prompted security concerns.
